metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:start_session

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (with a PID-disambiguation caveat); driver_capability: gap:start_session

## Scenario signal

multiple-sessions-same-cwd asserts: (1) two distinct session rows appear, each with its own `session_id` and PID; (2) each session's state transitions independently — no flapping between rows; (3) the PID-to-session binding is stable and does not cross-contaminate; (4) final state `ready`. The load-bearing signal is TWO distinct sessions in ONE cwd that never merge or flap.

## agent = yes

Mistral Vibe (live CLI v2.19.0 at ~/.local/bin/vibe) mints a fresh `session_<ts>_<hash>` directory on every launch and appends to `~/.vibe/logs/session/<that-dir>/messages.jsonl`. Two concurrent `vibe` REPLs launched in the SAME cwd therefore write to TWO DISTINCT session directories — the id is keyed by launch timestamp+hash, not by cwd. Nothing in Vibe serializes or collapses concurrent same-cwd sessions; each is an independent process with its own transcript + meta.json sidecar. So the agent trivially supports the scenario.

## daemon = full (identity is transcript-based, so rows structurally cannot flap)

The vibe adapter (commit e943e261) declares `Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.vibe/logs/session, SessionIDFromPath: sessionIDFromPath}` where `sessionIDFromPath` returns the `session_<...>` PARENT DIRECTORY (agent.go:56-65). Two concurrent sessions in one cwd → two directories → two `messages.jsonl` files → the fswatcher mints TWO distinct daemon sessions, each tailed by a FRESH stateful `Parser{}` via `JSONLineParser.NewParser` (agent.go:34). Because session identity derives from the directory and NOT from the PID or cwd, the two rows are unambiguous and cannot merge or flap into each other — this is a STRONGER isolation than claudecode's (whose flap risk came from PID-based reconciliation). Spec bullets (1) two distinct session rows and (2) independent transitions / no flapping are fully and unambiguously captured: the per-file tailer means a turn on session A's transcript only updates session A's metrics; there is no shared cwd state on the session object.

## The PID-to-session bind is ambiguous — a CAVEAT, not a bug

Bullet (3) 'each with its own PID / binding does not cross-contaminate' is the one degraded detail. `DiscoverPID` (pid.go:16) resolves a session's PID via `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine(processCmdPattern, cwd, disambiguate)`, where cwd comes from the meta.json sidecar. Two vibe processes in the SAME cwd BOTH match the command-line regex AND the cwd, so `narrowByCWD` (discovery.go) collects TWO matches and falls through to `disambiguate(matches)` — the shared default picker (prefer-unclaimed-else-highest-PID). Vibe exposes NO per-PID→session mapping (its sidecar has `working_directory`/`active_model`/`context_tokens` but no PID, sidecar.go:55-65), so there is nothing to break the tie: both distinct transcript-sessions can resolve to the SAME PID, or to each other's PID. Crucially this does NOT cause row flapping or identity confusion (identity is the directory, fixed at birth) — it only means the auxiliary PID attribute attached to a row may be shared or wrong. Per the assess rules (Caveats over downgrades: the canonical spec — two distinct, independent, non-flapping sessions — is met; a narrow attribute is gappy) this stays `daemon=full` with the PID-disambiguation caveat below, NOT `daemon=bug`: no trace is mis-handled; vibe's transport simply carries no PID→session key, and the daemon does the best a cwd+cmdline heuristic can. (Contrast claudecode, whose pid.go was HARDENED in #109/#169 to return 0-and-retry rather than guess when two same-cwd sessions are ambiguous — a future vibe hardening could mirror that, but it is not required for this cell's spec.)

None of the other known vibe daemon gaps touch this scenario's bullets: no per-turn cost (sidecar keeps only last-turn tokens); context WINDOW unresolved (mistral models absent from core/pkg/capacity — no bar asserted here); per-event timestamps backfilled to scan time; no Control/backchannel; no subagent linking (vibe subagents are in-process, not file-based). All caveats/follow-ups, not spec-required observables here.

## driver = gap:start_session

The interactive driver `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` is freshly scaffolded with `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` (line 113): only `send`/`slash`/`sleep` step arms are real. This scenario's recipe (mirroring claudecode's) needs THREE unported primitives: `start_session` (line 194 → `not_implemented`; the scenario-DEFINING primitive — launch a 2nd concurrent session while the first stays alive), `wait_turn` (line 169 stub; already the blocker for the base session-start cell), and per-step `session` targeting (line 195 stub; routes `{"session":1}` back to session 1). Plus the base SEAMs the driver shares: SEAM 1 (the launch command execs `mistral-vibe`, which is NOT on PATH — only `vibe` is) and SEAM 3 (`launch_repl` never captures the transcript path into `SES_TRANSCRIPT[$ACTIVE]`, so `emit_session_contract` records no session). The headline gap is `start_session` because it is what distinguishes this cell from the already-gapped session-start baseline. `record` ports SEAM 1 + `wait_turn` + `start_session` + `session` + SEAM 3 from the reference (claudecode) driver before it can drive. This is tooling work, not an observability limit — the cell keeps its real recipe + spec.
caveats: PID-to-session bind is ambiguous for two same-cwd vibe processes: both match the cmdline regex AND the cwd, so narrowByCWD falls through to the default disambiguator (prefer-unclaimed-else-highest-PID) with no PID->session key to break the tie (the meta.json sidecar carries no PID). The two DISTINCT transcript-sessions may therefore show the SAME PID or a swapped PID. This never causes row flapping or identity confusion (identity is the session_<...> directory, fixed at birth) — only the auxiliary PID attribute is imprecise. Anchor the spec's independence assertions on the working->ready transitions, NOT on PID equality; assert distinct session_ids (guaranteed) rather than distinct PIDs (not guaranteed).

Unlike claudecode (hardened in #109/#169 to return 0-and-retry when two same-cwd sessions are ambiguous), vibe's DiscoverPID guesses via the default disambiguator. A future hardening could mirror claudecode's strict-or-zero rule, but it is not required for this cell's spec (which asserts distinct session identity + independent arcs, both unaffected).

Provisional on live drive: daemon=full is derived from reading the adapter + shared scanner; no vibe recording has confirmed the two-distinct-session arc or the PID-ambiguity behavior end-to-end. record promotes this from provisional to settled.

The daemon emits no per-turn cost for vibe, and the mistral model's context WINDOW is unresolved (absent from core/pkg/capacity). Neither is asserted by this scenario; the spec asserts tokens_nonzero + model only.

sources.note: Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.vibe/logs/session, SessionIDFromPath: sessionIDFromPath, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser: () => &Parser{}}}. sessionIDFromPath returns the session_<...> parent DIRECTORY, so two concurrent same-cwd sessions -> two directories -> two distinct daemon sessions, each with a fresh stateful Parser. Identity is directory-based, not PID/cwd-based, so rows cannot flap.

DiscoverPID resolves PID via processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine(processCmdPattern, cwd, disambiguate) with cwd from the sidecar. Two same-cwd vibe processes both match cmdline+cwd -> the disambiguate callback picks one; there is no PID->session key to break the tie.

narrowByCWD: when >1 PID shares the cwd it calls disambiguate(matches) (default: highest PID). No metadata filter for vibe (contrast claudecode's claimedByOthers strict-or-zero). This is the exact seam that makes the PID attach ambiguous for two same-cwd vibe sessions.

sidecarState carries only cwd (working_directory), model (active_model), contextTokens (context_tokens) — NO pid field. Confirms there is no PID->session mapping in vibe's transport to disambiguate two same-cwd processes.

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" (line 113). start_session (line 194), wait_turn (line 169), and per-step session targeting (line 195) all dispatch to not_implemented. SEAM 1 (line ~155) execs mistral-vibe (not on PATH) and SEAM 3 (line ~158) skips transcript-path capture. record ports these from the claudecode reference driver.

Reference spec: anchor s1's first phase on the WORKING transition (relative_to start) to skip the proc-<PID> presession row that is born ready but never enters working; s2_birth carries new_session:true; s1 is re-driven while s2 is alive (same_session_as s1_turn1_start) to prove the binding stayed stable. Vibe spec mirrors this structure with mistral observations.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (~/.local/bin/vibe, v2.19.0) and authenticated (Mistral API key)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)

driver seams ported by record before driving: SEAM 1 launch binary vibe (not mistral-vibe), wait_turn poll on messages.jsonl, start_session (launch a concurrent session keeping the first alive), per-step session targeting, SEAM 3 transcript-path capture
no other vibe REPL is running in the staging cwd
setup: Driver auto-starts session 1 (slot 1) in <staging>/cwd; vibe mints session_<ts>_<hash> dir 1
The start_session step launches session 2 (slot 2) in the SAME <staging>/cwd; vibe mints a DISTINCT session_<ts>_<hash> dir 2 (id is timestamp+hash-keyed, not cwd-keyed)
Steps carrying {"session":1} route send/wait_turn back to session 1 while session 2 stays alive — exercising the concurrent, independent arcs

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alpha
Reply with exactly the word: bravo
Reply with exactly the word: alpha2

verify: events.jsonl contains 2 distinct session_<ts>_<hash> session_ids, both keyed to the same cwd slug
each session_id has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other
session 1 shows TWO working->ready arcs (turn1 + the post-start_session re-drive); session 2 shows ONE — proving session 1 stayed a distinct row while session 2 was alive
no row flapping: the two directory-keyed session rows never merge (PID attach may be shared/ambiguous — do NOT assert distinct PIDs)
meta.json sidecars supply model mistral-medium-3.5 and a non-zero context_tokens on turn_done for each session
